caption: Network Activity
description: Network Activity events report network connection and traffic activity.
name: network_activity

1.caption: Open
1.description: A new network connection was opened.

3.caption: Reset
3.description: The network connection was abnormally terminated or closed by a middle device like firewalls.

4.caption: Fail
4.description: The network connection failed. For example a connection timeout or no route to host.

5.caption: Refuse
5.description: The network connection was refused. For example an attempt to connect to a server port which is not open.

7.caption: Listen
7.description: A network endpoint began listening for new network connections.

dst_endpoint.description: The destination endpoint of the network connection — typically the responder. In bi-flow or asymmetric flow scenarios, this endpoint may represent the true initiator of the original connection. Use initiator_id to determine which endpoint actually initiated the communication.

initiator.description: The normalized caption of initiator_id. One of: Source Endpoint, Destination Endpoint, Unknown, or Other.

initiator_id.description: Indicates which endpoint initiated the network communication. Use this field to disambiguate source and destination roles when the event source represents a bi-directional flow as two separate unidirectional flows, or when a network appliance did not observe the connection handshake and has arbitrarily assigned endpoints. 1 (Source Endpoint) means src_endpoint is the true initiator and the assignment is correct. 2 (Destination Endpoint) means dst_endpoint is the true initiator and the assignment is reversed — the response side appears as the source. 0 (Unknown) means the initiator cannot be determined. Supersedes is_src_dst_assignment_known.

@deprecated.message: Use initiator_id instead, which provides richer semantics for bi-flow and asymmetric flow scenarios.

src_endpoint.description: The source endpoint of the network connection — typically the initiator. In bi-flow or asymmetric flow scenarios, this endpoint may represent the response side of a connection originally initiated by the destination. Use initiator_id to determine which endpoint actually initiated the communication.

url.description: The URL details relevant to the network traffic.
